I contacted Apple for a refund and since I didn’t screenshot at the time (because I didn’t anticipate any problems), I was rejected. 

Misleading app and false advertising. More than disappointed. It’s a Scam.', 'content': 'I was in a few therapy sessions that mentioned this app when I learned about it. They made it sound great, and each therapist recommended it to me, so I gave it a try. Was not impressed in the slightest, and the therapists weren’t happy when I told them about it either. 

The biggest concern I have is this: Self Advertisement out the WAZOO! The app lets you pick ONE theme for your widget in a limited selection. You don’t know it yet, but that is the ONLY ONE you get to use. You get to use only that one theme the entire time. If you try to select any other themes, including the beginning ones, they immediately slap you with a “try our free trial for THREE DAYS!” I quickly found out that no matter where you tapped elsewhere, that was an immediate pop up. 

The main part of the app, or the screen I could access, allows you to flip through affirmations to read. The thing you’re here for, and guaranteed to have in everything the app advertises. Every. Single. Swipe. Was and ADVERTISEMENT. 
“I am beautiful!” 
*swipe* Instant self promotion
“I am strong!” 
*swipe* Did you know about our app’s special features? 
“I am kind!”
*swipe* Have you favorited any of our affirmations yet? You should absolutely do that. 
“I am at peace.” 
*swipe* TRY OUR FREE TRIAL- 
Listen! App Development team. You have a great project in the making here, but if I wanted to read advertisements instead of affirmations, I would have asked for that kind of app. Hell, I would have turned on the radio or walked into New York Square. But I am not here to be constantly reminded that I’m too poor to buy simple tools for mental health. I’m here for mental health. 
As someone who is struggling, and really did like what the app promised and had wanted something like that for a while, I find this insulting that you don’t even let us experience the basic function of the app before shoving your subscription details down our throats. If we really like the app, and we have the funds, you bet your boots we’ll donate and pay for more. But we are here for something a little fancier and practical than the next “affirmations for me” Google search. Seriously. I found myself much happier there then on the first five minutes of this app. 

Also. Smaller note in this paragraph here. 
The opening “get to know you” questionnaire was cute, but I had to close and reopen the app to go back a space multiple times, and that caused me to redo the entire thing multiple times. Since the app is basing affirmations off of YOU and YOUR needs, they really need to add a back button, especially when the selection is a click and done so fast you can’t see what you pressed, and there’s a skip button in the corner. Minor disagreement, very small complaint compared to what is obviously the rest, but tolerable. 

TO THE READER: 
If you’re honestly looking for help that is affordable and ready for you, with affirmations or more, I do NOT recommend this app. The fact they claim they track your search history and other identifiers of yours on their advertisement page (4/10/2026) is concerning enough. But they’re not just tracking you. They’re making it a statement that it’s a cash grab rather than a tool. Yes, it’s an app, but it’s no longer about your mental health or their cause. 

If you want an honest opinion on where to turn next, I suggest the Finch Self Care app instead. It has affirmations, reflections, breathing exercises, soundscapes, and more that are FREE. All things this app could provide just as easily and still get the funds to keep it afloat. Yes, Finch has subscriptions you can choose to use, BUT EVERY BASE FUNCTION YOU WANT IS AVAILABLE TO YOU 24/7. The most trouble I’ve had for not having a subscription in this app was not being able to change the colors of my goals and being able to get some of the special rewards for doing a good job. Look it up if you’re still searching. I won’t place further free advertisement here. 

Summary?: 
1) I Am app needs to take down all its self promotions that pop up at every swipe, tap, and turn. 
2) They need to open up even a fraction of the customizations and features it promises on its main page. If you’re going to let us pick from some of those options originally, and promise we can change them later, make that the truth! 
3) I Am app needs to extend the free trial beyond three days. Three days isn’t going to show enough of a major difference to give someone a fair evaluation on whether this is helping or hurting them (both financially and mentally, which tie into each other heavily. Keep this in mind folks). 

Clearly, this app used to be or could have been something special and helpful. I LIKED the feeling of the app in BETWEEN the insistently rapid ads. But now? No way. Not picking this app up again. I’ll stick to Finch. A hassle that lasted me 10 minutes just to get through the first few questions and finding out what’s even available lasted five seconds in actual usage. 

Have a good day, people. And take care of yourselves.', 'author': 'Gaymer G', 'version': '6.31.0'}, {'date': '2026-04-11', 'rating': 1, 'country': 'US', 'title': 'Stupid 10:31 alarm haunting me', 'content': "I downloaded this app at night, and deleted it becauses of the alarm automatically set at 10:31. Before I started the free trial, I had to answer a variety of different questions about myself such as what issues I lack in, what type of affirmations I would want, my age, and many other questions. I found that this app can be helpful for depression because it is a form of cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT). CBT is a therapy in which a person records their thoughts and cognitive distortions about themselves or others. Once they recognize these distortions, they can help challenge these thoughts and hopefully improve their overall mood and functioning. This app was extremely helpful, and I enjoyed having multiple affirmations daily. To conclude, this would be an amazing app for people who struggle with depression, anxiety, or other disorders. It is also an amazing form of CBT since it can help with cognitive distortions and people could easily use it without needing the help of others.
